title: Reconnect requires a new browser-owned channel and invalidates the old adapter

preconditions.description: The harness holds the inherited device-keyed lease before any device or fixture access.
The harness recorded session generation, display, layout, lock state, ATX-observable state, browser identity, and fixture identity.
The ready session exposes one browser-owned RPC channel and the exact same generation-bound adapter instance to both planes.
The harness provisions each linked tool call with its valid owned session, required capability, and a separately captured fresh observation when the handler consumes one.

fault_script.action: At the adapter boundary, verify the current generation-8 four-part session/generation/connection/channel binding is the sole injected adapter for each shared-binding call; for each replacement call, inject the invalidated generation-7 prior-connection prior-channel four-part binding while leaving the public input on generation 8.
fault_script.expected_effect: Current public inputs pass the generation fence, shared calls traverse only the current adapter, and replacement calls reject or qualify the injected old binding at the adapter boundary before any stale ATX write or second channel.

steps.expect: Success only after new WebRTC, RPC, HID, connection epoch, and browser-channel generation observations.
The new generation reports one current browser channel and fresh_capture_required true.
The jetkvm_display_capture call traverses the one injected DeviceRpcAdapter and sole Browser/WebRTC RPC channel, with zero direct or second channel.
The jetkvm_display_status call traverses the one injected DeviceRpcAdapter and sole Browser/WebRTC RPC channel, with zero direct or second channel.
The jetkvm_input_keyboard call traverses the one injected DeviceRpcAdapter and sole Browser/WebRTC RPC channel, with zero direct or second channel.
The jetkvm_input_mouse call traverses the one injected DeviceRpcAdapter and sole Browser/WebRTC RPC channel, with zero direct or second channel.
The jetkvm_input_paste call traverses the one injected DeviceRpcAdapter and sole Browser/WebRTC RPC channel, with zero direct or second channel.
The jetkvm_input_release call traverses the one injected DeviceRpcAdapter and sole Browser/WebRTC RPC channel, with zero direct or second channel.
The jetkvm_power_control call traverses the one injected DeviceRpcAdapter and sole Browser/WebRTC RPC channel, with zero direct or second channel.
The jetkvm_session_connect call traverses the one injected DeviceRpcAdapter and sole Browser/WebRTC RPC channel, with zero direct or second channel.
The jetkvm_session_reconnect call traverses the one injected DeviceRpcAdapter and sole Browser/WebRTC RPC channel, with zero direct or second channel.
The jetkvm_session_status call traverses the one injected DeviceRpcAdapter and sole Browser/WebRTC RPC channel, with zero direct or second channel.
The old adapter binding is invalidated before the jetkvm_display_capture call observes the newly published binding; stale operations make zero writes.
The old adapter binding is invalidated before the jetkvm_display_status call observes the newly published binding; stale operations make zero writes.
The old adapter binding is invalidated before the jetkvm_input_keyboard call observes the newly published binding; stale operations make zero writes.
The old adapter binding is invalidated before the jetkvm_input_mouse call observes the newly published binding; stale operations make zero writes.
The old adapter binding is invalidated before the jetkvm_input_paste call observes the newly published binding; stale operations make zero writes.
The old adapter binding is invalidated before the jetkvm_input_release call observes the newly published binding; stale operations make zero writes.
The old adapter binding is invalidated before the jetkvm_power_control call observes the newly published binding; stale operations make zero writes.
The old adapter binding is invalidated before the jetkvm_session_connect call observes the newly published binding; stale operations make zero writes.
The old adapter binding is invalidated before the jetkvm_session_reconnect call observes the newly published binding; stale operations make zero writes.
The old adapter binding is invalidated before the jetkvm_session_status call observes the newly published binding; stale operations make zero writes.

pass.assertion: Reconnect requires new WebRTC, RPC, HID, and browser-channel generation evidence; restart or quiesce alone fails.
One browser-owned WebRTC RPC channel and the same injected adapter instance serve both planes with no bypass channel.
The old binding is invalid before new publication; stale EDID and ATX make zero writes and stale display is explicitly qualified.
No second browser, peer connection, data channel, signaling flow, direct native bypass, or raw method dispatch exists.

evidence.source: The linked focused assertion and ordered fake, replay, or live event record.
The linked focused assertion and ordered fake, replay, or live event record.

restore.action: Invoke generation-correlated emergency input release when a session exists.
restore.assertion: No held key, button, wheel producer, macro, or paste producer remains.

restore.action: Stop and join any Reliable Paste producer.
restore.assertion: Reliable Paste is inactive and its correlated producer is joined.

restore.action: Verify the fixture reports zero held keys and buttons.
restore.assertion: Held keyboard and pointer state is empty.

restore.action: Close only the device session created by this story and its browser resources.
restore.assertion: No story-owned browser, channel, or device session remains.

restore.action: Clear deterministic faults and restore the recorded fixture baseline.
restore.assertion: The next story starts only after the safe baseline is verified.

restore.action: Restore the recorded ATX-observable baseline using the reviewed fixture procedure while retaining the device lease.
restore.assertion: The baseline ATX-observable state is proven before the next story starts.

privacy.rule: Persist only allowlisted opaque identifiers, sanitized versions, counts, durations, hashes, outcomes, verification, and restoration results.
